name: Spec & Contract Check Before Code
description: Enforces spec-before-code: verifies requirements/spec and API contract exist before allowing implementation code to be written.

then.prompt:
Check .compliance-state.json → currentPhase. This hook applies from Foundation phase onward. If Setup phase, skip silently.

Before writing implementation code, verify:

1. REQUIREMENTS: Check if a requirements/spec document exists for the current feature (look in .kiro/specs/ or project docs).
2. API CONTRACT: If writing controller/endpoint code, check if an API contract (OpenAPI spec) exists for this module.
3. DOMAIN MODEL: If writing application/infrastructure layer code, check if the domain layer exists first.
4. LAYER ORDER: Verify correct layer order: domain → application → infrastructure → presentation → tests.

If any are missing, issue a WARNING explaining what should exist first. Reference: design precedes implementation, contracts precede controllers.

Do NOT block the operation. Warn only.
If all checks pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks above, append a JSON event to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl` (create the file if it doesn't exist). Format:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "pre-code-spec-check", "trigger": "preToolUse", "ruleId": "{primary-rule-checked}", "ruleSeverity": "{severity}", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{one-line-finding}"}
Log ONE event per rule checked.
